          (function () {
            var path = window.location.pathname || '';
            var root = document.documentElement;
            // 跟随系统亮暗：blog 聚合页 + 指定详情页（如 kimi-k3）。
            var isSystemThemeBlogHub = /(^|\/)blog\/?$/.test(path);
            var isSystemThemeBlogDetail = /(^|\/)blog\/(kimi-k3)(\/|$)/.test(path);
            if (isSystemThemeBlogHub || isSystemThemeBlogDetail) {
              var prefersDark = false;
              try {
                prefersDark = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
              } catch (e) {}
              if (window.INJECT_ENV && window.INJECT_ENV.theme === 'dark') {
                prefersDark = true;
              }
              root.classList.remove('dark', 'light');
              root.classList.add(prefersDark ? 'dark' : 'light');
              return;
            }
            // 其余 blog 路径：维持强制深色。
            var isBlogPath = /(^|\/)blog(\/|$)/.test(path);
            if (isBlogPath) {
              root.classList.remove('light');
              root.classList.add('dark');
            }
          })();

[list-style:none] min-w-0 shrink"><a class="vp-raw transition-colors hover:text-(--Labels-Primary) whitespace-nowrap" href="/blog/"><span class="block overflow-hidden min-w-0 whitespace-nowrap shrink text-ellipsis">Research</span></a></li></ol></nav></div><section class="pb-20 w-full"><div class="max-w-none markdown"><h1 node="[object Object]">Introducing Kimi K2 Thinking</h1>
<p>Today, we are introducing <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Kimi</strong> <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">K2</strong> <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Thinking</strong>, our best open-source thinking model.</p>
<p>Built as a <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">thinking agent</strong>, it reasons step by step <em class="italic text-(--Labels-Secondary)">while</em> using tools, achieving state-of-the-art performance on Humanity's Last Exam (HLE), BrowseComp, and other benchmarks, with major gains in reasoning, agentic search, coding, writing, and general capabilities.</p>
<p>Kimi K2 Thinking can execute up to <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">200 – 300 sequential tool calls</strong> without human interference, reasoning coherently across hundreds of steps to solve complex problems.</p>
<p>It marks our latest efforts in <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">test-time scaling</strong>, by scaling both thinking tokens and tool calling steps.</p>
<p>K2 Thinking is now live on <a href="https://www.kimi.com/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline">kimi.com</a> under the chat mode <a href="#footnote-1" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline">[1]</a>, with its full agentic mode available soon. It is also accessible through the Kimi K2 Thinking <a href="https://platform.kimi.ai/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">API</a>.</p>
<h3 node="[object Object]">Evaluations</h3>
<p>Kimi K2 Thinking sets new records across benchmarks that assess reasoning, coding, and agent capabilities. K2 Thinking achieves 44.9% on HLE with tools, 60.2% on BrowseComp, and 71.3% on SWE-Bench Verified, demonstrating strong generalization as a state-of-the-art thinking agent model.</p>

<h3 node="[object Object]">Agentic Reasoning</h3>
<p>K2 Thinking demonstrates outstanding reasoning and problem-solving abilities. On Humanity’s Last Exam (HLE)—a rigorously crafted, closed‑ended benchmark—spanning thousands of expert‑level questions across more than 100 subjects, K2 Thinking achieved <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">a​</strong> ​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">state-of-the-art</strong>​ ​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">​score of</strong> <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">​​44.9%</strong>, ​ with search, python, and web-browsing tools, establishing new records in multi‑domain expert‑level reasoning performance.</p>
<div class="jsx-7973383911a9d337" style="visibility: hidden;"><div class="jsx-7973383911a9d337 comparison-chart"><div class="jsx-7973383911a9d337 comparison-detail"><div class="jsx-7973383911a9d337 chart-item coding"><div class="jsx-7973383911a9d337 chart-item-content"><div class="jsx-654492993 chart-container"><div class="jsx-654492993 chart-h4">Humanity's Last Exam (Text-only) w/ tools <a href="#footnote-3-2" class="jsx-654492993">[3.b]</a></div><div class="jsx-654492993 chart-h6">Expert-level questions across subjects</div></div></div></div></div></div></div>
<p>By reasoning while actively using a diverse set of tools, K2 Thinking is capable of <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">planning, reasoning, executing, and adapting across hundreds of steps</strong> to tackle some of the most challenging academic and analytical problems. In one instance, it successfully solved a <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">PhD-level mathematics problem</strong> through ​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">23 interleaved reasoning and tool calls</strong>​, exemplifying its capacity for deep, structured reasoning and long-horizon problem solving:</p>

<h3 node="[object Object]">Agentic Coding</h3>
<p>K2 Thinking exhibits substantial gains in coding and software development tasks. It achieves scores of 61.1% on SWE-Multilingual, 71.3% on SWE-Bench Verified, and 47.1% on Terminal-Bench, showcasing strong generalization across programming languages and agent scaffolds.</p>
<p>The model delivers notable improvements on HTML, React, and component-intensive front-end tasks—translating ideas into fully functional, responsive products. In agentic coding settings, it reasons while invoking tools, integrating fluidly into software agents to execute complex, multi-step development workflows with precision and adaptability.</p>

<h3 node="[object Object]">Agentic Search and Browsing</h3>
<p>K2 Thinking demonstrates strong performance in agentic search and browsing scenarios. On BrowseComp—a challenging benchmark designed to evaluate models' ability to ​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">continuously browse, search, and reason over hard-to-find real-world web information</strong>​—K2 Thinking achieved a score of <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">​​60.​2%</strong>, significantly outperforming the human baseline of 29.2%. This result highlights K2 Thinking's superior capability for goal-directed, web-based reasoning and its robustness in dynamic, information-rich environments.</p>
<p>K2 Thinking can execute ​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">200–300 sequential tool calls</strong>​, driven by <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">long-horizon planning</strong> and ​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">adaptive reasoning</strong>​. It performs dynamic cycles of ​<em class="italic text-(--Labels-Secondary)">think → search → browser use → think → code</em>​, continually generating and refining hypotheses, verifying evidence, reasoning, and constructing coherent answers. This interleaved reasoning allows it to decompose ambiguous, open-ended problems into clear, actionable subtasks.</p>

<h3 node="[object Object]">General Capabilities</h3>
<p><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Creative Writing: ​</strong>K2 Thinking delivers improvements in completeness and richness. It shows stronger command of style and instruction, handling diverse tones and formats with natural fluency. Its writing becomes more vivid and imaginative—poetic imagery carries deeper associations, while stories and scripts feel more human, emotional, and purposeful. The ideas it expresses often reach greater thematic depth and resonance.</p>
<p><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Practical Writing: ​</strong>K2 Thinking demonstrates marked gains in reasoning depth, perspective breadth, and instruction adherence. It follows prompts with higher precision, addressing each requirement clearly and systematically—often expanding on every mentioned point to ensure thorough coverage. In academic, research, and long-form analytical writing, it excels at producing rigorous, logically coherent, and substantively rich content, making it particularly effective in scholarly and professional contexts.</p>
<p><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Personal & Emotional: ​</strong>When addressing personal or emotional questions, K2 Thinking responds with more empathy and balance. Its reflections are thoughtful and specific, offering nuanced perspectives and actionable next steps. It helps users navigate complex decisions with clarity and care—grounded, practical, and genuinely human in tone.</p>
<div style="min-height: 1150px;"></div>
<h3 node="[object Object]">Inference Efficiency</h3>
<p>Low-bit quantization is an effective way to reduce inference latency and GPU memory usage on large-scale inference servers. However, thinking models use excessive decoding lengths, and thus quantization often results in substantial performance drops.</p>
<p>To overcome this challenge, we adopt Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) during the post-training phase, applying INT4 weight-only quantization to the MoE components. It allows K2 Thinking to support native INT4 inference with a roughly 2x generation speed improvement while achieving state-of-the-art performance. All benchmark results are reported under INT4 precision.</p>

<p><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Footnotes</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>To ensure a fast, lightweight experience, we selectively employ a subset of tools and reduce the number of tool call turns under the chat mode on <a href="https://www.kimi.com/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline">kimi.com</a>. As a result, chatting on <a href="https://www.kimi.com/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline">kimi.com</a> may not reproduce our benchmark scores. Our agentic mode will be updated soon to reflect the full capabilities of K2 Thinking.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a></li>
<li><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Testing Details:</strong> <a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
a. All benchmarks were evaluated at temperature = 1.0 and 256 k context length for K2 Thinking, except for SciCode, for which we followed the official temperature setting of 0.0.
b. HLE (no tools), AIME25, HMMT25, and GPQA were capped at a 96k thinking-token budget, while IMO-Answer Bench, LiveCodeBench and OJ-Bench were capped at a 128k thinking-token budget. Longform Writing was capped at a 32k completion-token budget.
c. For AIME and HMMT (no tools), we report the average of 32 runs (avg@32). For AIME and HMMT (with Python), we report the average of 16 runs (avg@16). For IMO-AnswerBench, we report the average of 8 runs (avg@8).</li>
<li><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Baselines:</strong> <a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
a. GPT-5, Claude-4.5-sonnet, Grok-4 results and DeepSeek-V3.2 results are quoted from the <a href="https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">GPT-5 post</a>, <a href="https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-for-developers/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">GPT-5 for Developers post</a>, <a href="https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-system-card/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">GPT-5 system card</a>, <a href="https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-4-5" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">claude-sonnet-4-5</a>, <a href="https://x.ai/news/grok-4" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">grok-4</a>, <a href="https://api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news250929/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">deepseek-v3.2</a>, the public <a href="https://www.tbench.ai/leaderboard" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Terminal-Bench leaderboard</a> (Terminus-2), the public <a href="https://www.vals.ai/benchmarks/legal_bench" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Vals AI leaderboard</a> and the <a href="https://artificialanalysis.ai/" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">artificialanalysis</a>. Benchmarks for which no available public scores were re-tested under the same conditions used for k2 thinking and are marked with an asterisk(*). For the GPT-5 test, we set the reasoning effort to high. <a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
b. The GPT-5 and Grok-4 on the HLE full set with tools are 35.2 and 38.6 from their official posts. In our internal evaluation on the HLE text-only subset, GPT-5 scores <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">41.7</strong> and Grok-4 scores ​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">38.6 ​</strong>​(Grok-4’s launch cited <strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">41.0</strong> on the text-only subset). For GPT-5's HLE text-only w/o tool, we use score from <a href="https://labs.scale.com/leaderboard/humanitys_last_exam_text_only" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Scale.ai</a>, and the official GPT-5 score on the HLE full set (no tools) is 24.8. <a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
c. For <a href="https://aclanthology.org/2025.emnlp-main.1794.pdf" class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">IMO-AnswerBench</a>: GPT-5 scored 65.6 in the benchmark paper. We re-evaluated GPT-5 with official API and obtained a score of 76. <a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a></li>
<li><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">For HLE (w/ tools) and the agentic-search benchmarks:</strong> <a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
a. K2 Thinking was equipped with search, code-interpreter, and web-browsing tools.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
b. BrowseComp-ZH, Seal-0, FinSearchComp-T3 were run 4 times independently and the average is reported (avg@4).<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
c. The evaluation used o3-mini as judge, configured identically to the official HLE setting; judge prompts were taken verbatim from the official repository.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
d. On HLE, the maximum step limit was 120, with a 48 k-token reasoning budget per step; on agentic-search tasks, the limit was 300 steps with a 24 k-token reasoning budget per step.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
e. When tool execution results cause the accumulated input to exceed the model's context limit (256k), we employ a simple context management strategy that hides all previous tool outputs.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
f. The web access to Hugging Face may lead to data leakage in certain benchmark tests, such as HLE. K2 Thinking can achieve a score of 51.3 on HLE without blocking Hugging Face. To ensure a fair and rigorous comparison, we blocked access to Hugging Face during testing.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a></li>
<li><strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">For Coding Tasks:</strong><a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
a. Terminal-Bench scores were obtained with the default agent framework (Terminus-2) and the provided JSON parser.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
b. For other coding tasks, the result was produced with our in-house evaluation harness. The harness is derived from SWE-agent, but we clamp the context windows of the Bash and Edit tools and rewrite the system prompt to match the task semantics.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a>
c. All reported scores of coding tasks are averaged over 5 independent runs.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a></li>
<li>​<strong class="font-semibold text-(--Labels-Primary)">Heavy Mode</strong>​: K2 Thinking Heavy Mode employs an efficient parallel strategy: it first rolls out eight trajectories simultaneously, then reflectively aggregates all outputs to generate the final result. Heavy mode for GPT-5 denotes the official GPT-5 Pro score.<a class="text-(--Colors-KMBlue) hover:underline" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"></a></li>
